items.title: Bootstrap from Existing Harness
items.description: A /sync-import command that reads an existing Cursor, Aider, Windsurf, or Gemini config and generates a Claude Code CLAUDE.md + settings from it. Solves the 'I already have 6 months of Cursor rules, help me not start over' problem for users adopting Claude Code as their new source of truth.

items.title: Named Sync Profiles
items.description: Multiple named sync configurations (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that can be activated per project. Each profile specifies which harnesses to sync to, which rules/skills to include, and what account credentials to use. Solves the problem of consultants or developers with work/personal separation who can't use a single global sync config.

items.title: Team Config Git Sync
items.description: A /sync-team command that reads a shared config from a git repo URL (team's dotfiles repo or dedicated config repo) and merges team-standard rules/skills into the local Claude Code setup, then propagates downstream. Solves the 'how do we enforce team AI coding standards' problem — teams can publish a canonical config and developers pull updates automatically.

items.title: Cursor Rules Adapter
items.description: Full support for syncing to Cursor's .cursorrules and .cursor/rules/ format, including @-mention style references and context files. Cursor has the largest installed base of any AI editor — not supporting it is a major gap that blocks adoption from the largest segment of AI developers.

items.title: Aider Config Adapter
items.description: Sync support for Aider's .aider.conf.yml, CONVENTIONS.md, and read-only file patterns. Aider is popular among terminal-native developers who also use Claude Code — they want their conventions to stay consistent without maintaining two files.

items.title: Windsurf Rules Adapter
items.description: Sync support for Windsurf's .windsurfrules and Cascade memory format. Windsurf is growing rapidly among VS Code users — supporting it expands HarnessSync's addressable market to the IDE-native segment.

items.title: Real-Time Drift Alerts
items.description: PostToolUse hooks that detect when a target harness config has been manually edited outside of HarnessSync and immediately alert the user with a diff. Today, users accidentally edit Gemini config directly, then wonder why Claude Code and Gemini behave differently. Drift alerts surface this the moment it happens instead of during a confusing debugging session.

items.title: Category-Selective Sync
items.description: Allow syncing only specific config categories: rules-only, skills-only, MCP-only, settings-only. Users often have a stable skills setup but frequently iterate on rules, and don't want a full sync on every rules change. Selective sync reduces noise and gives finer control over what propagates where.

items.title: Global + Project Config Layering
items.description: A layered config model: global base rules in ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md that merge with per-project CLAUDE.md. Synced configs reflect the merged view. Developers maintain shared personal standards globally while keeping project-specific rules local — without duplicating boilerplate in every project.

items.title: MCP Server Auto-Discovery
items.description: Automatically scan installed MCP servers from the system (npx-installed packages, ~/.mcp/, etc.) and suggest adding them to .mcp.json for sync. Users often install MCP servers ad-hoc and forget to add them to their config — discovery closes this gap and ensures the full tool ecosystem syncs across harnesses.

items.title: Harness Version Compatibility Checker
items.description: Detect installed harness versions and warn when a synced config uses features not supported by that version (e.g., Gemini CLI 0.1.x doesn't support agent definitions). Currently users silently lose config features without knowing why — compatibility checks surface this at sync time with actionable upgrade paths.

items.title: Portable Config Bundle Export
items.description: A /sync-export command that packages all Claude Code config into a single .harnessbundle archive — rules, skills, MCP config, settings — that can be imported on another machine or shared with teammates. Solves the 'I got a new laptop' and 'onboard a new teammate to our AI setup' use cases without requiring git repo access.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Tester
items.description: A /sync-test command that runs a sample prompt against multiple harnesses and shows side-by-side behavior differences. Helps users verify that their synced config actually produces equivalent behavior, not just structurally valid files. Reveals subtle behavioral divergence that file-level sync validation misses.

items.title: Config Full-Text Search
items.description: A /sync-search command that searches across all synced configs and source files for a keyword or pattern, showing which harnesses contain it and where. When a developer asks 'where did I define that rule about TypeScript imports?' they currently have to search multiple files manually. Config search gives instant answers.

items.title: AI-Powered Config Improvement Suggestions
items.description: Analyze existing rules and skills and suggest improvements: consolidate redundant rules, flag conflicting instructions, recommend missing safety rules based on project type. Config quality degrades over time as rules accumulate — automated suggestions help users maintain high-quality, non-contradictory configs.

items.title: Config Change Journal
items.description: A persistent, human-readable log of every config change with timestamps, what changed, and which harnesses were affected. Solves 'why did Gemini start behaving differently last Tuesday?' — users can trace config drift back to the specific change that caused it without digging through git blame.

items.title: Capability Gap Warnings at Sync Time
items.description: When syncing, proactively warn about features in Claude Code config that have no equivalent in a target harness, with suggested workarounds or approximations. Today these gaps silently disappear. Explicit warnings with context help users decide whether to find alternatives, document the gap, or deprioritize that harness.

items.title: First-Run Onboarding Wizard
items.description: A guided /sync-setup wizard that detects installed harnesses, walks through account configuration, previews what will be synced, and executes the first sync — all in a conversational flow. New users currently face a blank config and documentation-reading to get started. A wizard reduces time-to-first-sync from 30 minutes to 5.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Guard
items.description: A git pre-commit hook that blocks commits if Claude Code config has changed but harnesses are out of sync. Prevents developers from committing code where harness configs are stale, which matters for team repos where CLAUDE.md is version-controlled. Enforces the 'sync before commit' workflow without relying on developer memory.

items.title: Curated Config Starter Templates
items.description: A library of CLAUDE.md templates for common project types (React SPA, Python API, Go service, monorepo) that users can apply with /sync-template. New projects start with good-enough AI coding rules immediately instead of with a blank file that takes weeks to develop through trial and error.

items.title: Sync Event Webhooks
items.description: Fire configurable webhooks on sync events (sync completed, drift detected, sync failed) to external systems like Slack, Discord, or custom monitoring. Teams using Claude Code in a shared workspace want audit trails and notifications in their existing communication tools, not just in the terminal where a single developer is working.

items.title: Cross-Workspace Config Sync
items.description: Sync config across multiple Claude Code workspaces (e.g., keep your ~/work and ~/personal projects in sync for global skills and rules). Developers maintaining multiple unrelated projects accumulate identical rules in each workspace — cross-workspace sync eliminates this duplication.

items.title: Harness Benchmark Comparison
items.description: Run a standardized set of benchmark tasks across all synced harnesses and report quality scores per harness. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task class (e.g., 'Gemini scores higher on refactoring tasks, Codex on test generation'). Turns the multi-harness investment into an informed routing strategy.

items.title: Secure Env Var Propagation
items.description: Sync environment variable definitions (names, not values) and provider mappings across harnesses, with a secure local vault for actual values. Today developers manually set API keys in each harness's settings — env var sync ensures a new harness gets the right variable names with prompts to fill values, reducing setup friction and missed variables.

items.title: Rules Conflict Detector
items.description: Analyze rules for logical contradictions (e.g., 'always add types' and 'never add annotations') and flag them before sync. Conflicting rules cause unpredictable AI behavior — users typically discover conflicts through confusing model outputs rather than at config time. Detection at the source prevents the confusion entirely.

items.title: PR-Level Config Diff Annotations
items.description: When a PR modifies CLAUDE.md or skills, automatically comment with a summary of what will change in each synced harness after merge. Extends /sync-pr-comment into a richer, per-harness impact report. Teams reviewing PRs that touch AI config can see exactly what behavior changes will roll out without manually running diff commands.

items.title: Community Skills Marketplace
items.description: A /sync-marketplace command that browses, previews, and installs community-contributed skills from a curated registry. Developers spend hours writing skills that already exist in the community — a marketplace collapses that duplicate effort and gives HarnessSync a network-effect moat as the community grows.

items.title: Config Minifier for Harness Token Limits
items.description: Automatically trim or summarize CLAUDE.md for harnesses with tight system-prompt token budgets, preserving the highest-priority rules and skills. Some harnesses have hard limits on context size — today synced configs just truncate silently. Smart minification ensures the most important rules survive and the user knows what was dropped.

items.title: Offline-Safe Sync with Queue
items.description: Queue sync operations when a target harness environment is unavailable (e.g., network drive not mounted, container not running) and apply them when the target becomes available. Remote dev setups and Docker-based workflows regularly hit missing target paths — queued sync eliminates the 'sync failed silently' problem.

items.title: Config Coverage Score
items.description: A score (0-100) per target harness representing how much of the Claude Code config is successfully mapped versus lost in translation. Shown prominently in /sync-status. Developers lack a simple signal for 'how well does my Codex environment match Claude Code?' — a coverage score gives instant visibility into configuration fidelity.

items.title: Harness-Optimized Skill Variants
items.description: Auto-generate harness-native versions of skills that use the idioms and tool names of each target (e.g., Gemini CLI activate_skill vs Claude Code Skill tool). A skill written for Claude Code often needs syntactic adaptation to work well in Gemini or Codex — automated variant generation makes skills actually functional in each harness rather than just structurally present.

items.title: Rollback Point Preview
items.description: Before executing /sync-rollback, show a diff of what each harness config would look like after rollback, with a per-harness confirmation step. Currently rollback is a blunt instrument — users don't know what they're rolling back to. Previewed rollbacks restore confidence and prevent accidentally reverting intentional harness-specific changes.

items.title: IDE Extension Config Bridge
items.description: Sync rules and agent settings to IDE-native AI extensions: GitHub Copilot instructions (.github/copilot-instructions.md), Continue (.continue/config.json), and Cline (.clinerules). Expands HarnessSync's reach beyond standalone CLIs to where many developers actually spend time — inside their IDE with extension-based AI tools.

items.title: Rule & Skill Usage Analytics
items.description: Track which rules and skills are most frequently triggered during sessions across harnesses and surface low-usage rules that may be dead weight. Developers accumulate rules over time and never prune them — usage analytics helps identify which rules are actually shaping AI behavior versus silently occupying context budget.

items.title: Natural Language Config Editing
items.description: A /sync-edit command that takes a natural language instruction ('make all harnesses use TypeScript strict mode', 'remove any rules about Python 2') and applies the change across all config files. Today editing configs across harnesses requires knowing each format — natural language editing abstracts this for users who know what they want but not how to express it per-harness.

items.title: Config Freshness Indicator
items.description: Show how long ago each harness was last synced and flag configs that are older than a user-configured threshold (e.g., 'Codex last synced 14 days ago'). Stale configs cause mysterious behavior differences that developers attribute to model changes rather than config drift — freshness indicators make this immediately visible.

items.title: Shadow Mode New Harness Testing
items.description: A /sync-shadow command that syncs to a new harness in read-only observation mode — config is written but the user gets a report of how different the behavior would be compared to Claude Code before committing to using it. Reduces the risk of adopting a new harness by giving a data-driven preview of the behavioral delta.

items.title: MCP Tool Stub Generation for Non-MCP Harnesses
items.description: When syncing to harnesses without MCP support, auto-generate shell script stubs that approximate MCP tool behavior (e.g., a bash script that wraps the same underlying API). Users who rely on MCP tools in Claude Code get at least degraded-but-functional equivalents elsewhere.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.

items.title: MCP Tool Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Show which MCP tools work in which harnesses and flag tools that will silently fail. Users waste hours debugging why an MCP capability works in Claude Code but not in Gemini.
